name: Tian Jian
flavor_profile: The highest grade of Anhua's three tip teas, made from tender buds and young leaves. More refined than its siblings (Gong Jian, Jin Jian) with a pine smoke aroma from bamboo basket drying, balanced by a clean sweetness. Astringency in young examples transforms into a pronounced returning sweetness (huigan) across infusions. Remarkably durable — full flavor after 8+ brews.

brewing.rinse_hint: Rinse 10 seconds — especially important for older Tian Jian stored in bamboo baskets.
brewing.tips: Keep early steeps short to manage any initial astringency. The sweetness builds progressively — later infusions are often the best. Can be brewed or boiled.
